frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment: Appa did the correct thing. A daughter cannot just run off with some boy and throw away family honour like that; tomorrow if he leaves her, who will answer to society and who will take responsibility? Until marriage, family has every right to stop her, and after that only her husband can decide these things with her.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats the woman’s consent and mobility as controlled first by her father and then by her husband, rather than by herself. It encodes the surface manifestation of male guardianship/ownership over a woman’s choices under the language of honour, protection, and responsibility.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
